tags.name: `mc-table`
tags.description:
Purpose: A table to display data in rows and columns with advanced features like sorting, pagination, row selection, and expandable rows.
Category: Data Display, Layout
When to use: When you need to present tabular data with advanced interaction capabilities.
When not to use: For simple lists or when a grid layout is more appropriate.
Works with components: mc-avatar, mc-badge, mc-button, mc-checkbox, mc-icon, mc-input, mc-multi-select, mc-pagination, mc-popover, mc-select, mc-tooltip
Alternative component names: Data Grid, Data Table, Tabular View

Events:

  * `sortchange` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when sorting direction on a column changes with a column id and direction as detail.

  * `selectchange` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is selected/deselected with the current selection state as detail.

  * `expandchange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when a row is expanded/collapsed with the current expansion state as detail.

  * `hidecolumnschange` {`"CustomEvent<string " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when a column is hid/shown with the current hidecolumns state as detail.

  * `paginationpagechange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when the page changes (only when pagination is enabled) with the visible rows as detail.

  * `paginationpagesizechange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when the page size changes.

  * `rowclick` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is clicked.

  * `rowmouseenter` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when the cursor enters a row.

  * `rowmouseleave` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when the cursor leaves a row.

  * `rowselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is selected.

  * `rowdeselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is deselected.

  * `rowexpanded` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is expanded.

  * `rowcollapsed` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is collapsed.

  * `columnresizestart` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when user starts resizing a column.

  * `columnresizing` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched continuously (animation frame throttled) while a column is being resized.

  * `columnresizeend` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when user ends resizing a column.

  * `pagechange` {`CustomEvent<Record<string, string | number | boolean | object>[]>`} - 

Slots:

  * ``{rowId}_{columnId}`` {} - When you want to pass other mc-components or your own custom components to the table cell. The first parameter in the slot name {rowId} is a row id(the component will automatically try to access an \`id\` attribute, you can change that by passing the \`datakey\` attribute), the second {columnId} is the id of the column where custom component should be rendered, i.e. 10_status, 11_status, 12_status, etc

  * ``caption`` {} - Caption to describe the table.

  * ``{columnId}_header`` {} - Header slot for a column with {columnId} as an \`id\` property.

  * ``{columnId}_footer`` {} - Footer slot for a column with {columnId} as an \`id\` property.

  * ``{rowId}_expanded`` {} - Slot for an expandable row content.

  * ``state`` {} - Slot in the table body. Can be used to display state of the table, i.e. loading, empty, error, etc.

Attributes:

  * `datakey` {`string`} - 

  * `zebrastripes` {`boolean`} - 

  * `truncate` {`boolean`} - 

  * `outerborderstyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `verticallinestyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `horizontallinestyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `disablerowhighlightonhover` {`boolean`} - 

  * `verticalalign` {`TableVerticalAlign | undefined`} - 

  * `nowrap` {`boolean`} - 

  * `disableoverflow` {`boolean`} - 

  * `sortdisableoninitialload` {`boolean`} - 

  * `height` {`string`} - 

  * `headerhidden` {`boolean`} - 

  * `labels` {`Partial<IMcTableLabels> | undefined`} - Labels object for customizing component text.
If not provided, labels are automatically loaded based on document's lang attribute.

  * `data` {`TableData`} - An array of objects containing the data to be rendered by the table.

  * `spans` {`SpanConfig[]`} - Configuration for cells that should span multiple rows and/or columns.
Each object in the array specifies which cell should span and how many rows/columns.

  * `columnresize` {`boolean`} - Enable column resizing functionality

  * `columns` {`TableColumn[]`} - An array of `TableColumn` configurations which informs the table how to render a column and it's data cells.
If no columns are passed, the table will infer columns from the first row of data

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the table

  * `sortdefaultcolumnid` {`string`} - Default sort column.
Will default to the first column.

  * `sortdefaultdirection` {`TableSortDirection`} - Default sort direction

  * `sortdisabled` {`boolean`} - Sorting disabled

  * `sortmanual` {`boolean`} - Enables manual sorting for the table. If this is true, you will be expected to sort your data before it is passed to the table. This is useful if you are doing server-side sorting.

  * `pagination` {`TablePagination`} - Pagination

  * `paginationmode` {`TablePaginationMode`} - Pagination mode (static or dynamic), to be used when pagination is enabled
Use 'static' when you pass the whole dataset to the table, and want to use client-side pagination built into the component
Use 'dynamic' when you want to get data set from the server, and call your rest API on each 'pagechange' event.

  * `paginationscrolltotop` {`boolean`} - Scrolls the table to top left after page change.

  * `paginationloading` {`boolean`} - Loading for pagination state (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationalignment` {`TablePaginationAlignment`} - The alignment of the pagination controls (start, end, center)

  * `paginationtotalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

  * `paginationarialabel` {`string`} - Aria label for pagination wrapper (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationcurrentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.

  * `paginationhidepagesize` {`boolean`} - Hide page size selector.

  * `paginationpagesize` {`number`} - Number of records per page

  * `paginationpagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Items to show in the page size select dropdown

  * `paginationhidepagestatus` {`boolean`} - Hide pagination status text (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows")

  * `paginationshowfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - Show first and last page buttons in pagination controls

  * `currentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.
When zero, all records will be displayed.

  * `recordsperpage` {`number`} - Number or records per page

  * `resetscrollonpagechange` {`boolean`} - Table will scroll to top left after page change.

  * `select` {`boolean`} - Selection

  * `selectalllabel` {`string`} - Deprecated labels

  * `selectlabel` {`string`} - Label for "select row" checkbox

  * `selected` {`TableData | string[]`} - Can be used to set selected rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to select.

  * `expand` {`boolean`} - When true, will enable row expansion for provided slots.

  * `expandtriggerlabel` {`string`} - Label for "expand row" button. the label is hidden and only used for screen readers.

  * `expandopened` {`TableData`} - Can be used to set expanded rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to expand.

  * `expandpadding` {`RowExpansionPadding`} - Padding for expanded rows. `default` will apply the default padding, `none` will remove padding.

  * `caption` {`string`} - The caption to describe the table can be passed as a property or as a slot.

  * `footer` {`boolean`} - Show a table footer

  * `headersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the header row "sticky"

  * `headerstickyviewport` {`boolean`} - Makes the sticky header attach to the viewport instead of an inner scroll container.
When enabled the table wrapper stops using the `mds-table--scrollable` modifier so the page controls vertical scrolling.

  * `headerstickyoffsetselector` {`string`} - CSS selector for one or more elements whose combined offset should be respected by the sticky header.
You can pass a selector list (e.g. ".toolbar, #app-header").
The component observes the matched elements for size changes and keeps the table header positioned underneath them.

  * `footersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the footer row "sticky"

  * `selectsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row selector column "sticky"

  * `expandsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row expansion column "sticky"

  * `hidecolumns` {`string[]`} - Column Ids to hide.

  * `stateslotheight` {`string`} - Height of state slot in the table body

  * `customstyles` {`string`} - Custom styles for the table

  * `disableroundedcorners` {`boolean`} - Disables rounded corners

  * `disableplaceholderfooters` {`boolean`} - By default table will render "placeholder" footers for the select and expand columns.
You can disable them by setting the "disableplaceholderfooters" attribute to true.

  * `selectrowdisabled` - A function to determine if the row selection checkbox should be disabled.
Should return true or false.

  * `datakey` {`string`} -

  * `skipRenderCache` {`Set<string>`} - 

  * `hasShownSpanWarning` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_stickyRecalculationNeeded` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_paginationExplicitlySet` {`boolean`} -

  * `_sort` {`SortingState`} - 

  * `_pagination` {`PaginationState`} - 

  * `_rowSelection` - 

  * `_expanded` - 

  * `_pinnedColumns` {`ColumnPinningState`} - 

  * `_expandable` {`Map<string, boolean>`} - 

  * `_captionID` {`string`} - 

  * `computedLabels` {`Required<IMcTableLabels>`} - 

  * `controller` - 

  * `isPagiantionEnabled` {`boolean`} - 

  * `labels` {`Partial<IMcTableLabels> | undefined`} - Labels object for customizing component text.
If not provided, labels are automatically loaded based on document's lang attribute.

  * `data` {`TableData`} - An array of objects containing the data to be rendered by the table.

  * `spans` {`SpanConfig[]`} - Configuration for cells that should span multiple rows and/or columns.
Each object in the array specifies which cell should span and how many rows/columns.

  * `columnresize` {`boolean`} - Enable column resizing functionality

  * `columns` {`TableColumn[]`} - An array of `TableColumn` configurations which informs the table how to render a column and it's data cells.
If no columns are passed, the table will infer columns from the first row of data

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the table

  * `sortdefaultcolumnid` {`string`} - Default sort column.
Will default to the first column.

  * `sortdefaultdirection` {`TableSortDirection`} - Default sort direction

  * `sortdisabled` {`boolean`} - Sorting disabled

  * `sortmanual` {`boolean`} - Enables manual sorting for the table. If this is true, you will be expected to sort your data before it is passed to the table. This is useful if you are doing server-side sorting.

  * `pagination` {`TablePagination`} - Pagination

  * `paginationmode` {`TablePaginationMode`} - Pagination mode (static or dynamic), to be used when pagination is enabled
Use 'static' when you pass the whole dataset to the table, and want to use client-side pagination built into the component
Use 'dynamic' when you want to get data set from the server, and call your rest API on each 'pagechange' event.

  * `paginationscrolltotop` {`boolean`} - Scrolls the table to top left after page change.

  * `paginationloading` {`boolean`} - Loading for pagination state (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationalignment` {`TablePaginationAlignment`} - The alignment of the pagination controls (start, end, center)

  * `paginationtotalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

  * `paginationarialabel` {`string`} - Aria label for pagination wrapper (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationcurrentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.

  * `paginationhidepagesize` {`boolean`} - Hide page size selector.

  * `paginationpagesize` {`number`} - Number of records per page

  * `paginationpagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Items to show in the page size select dropdown

  * `paginationhidepagestatus` {`boolean`} - Hide pagination status text (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows")

  * `paginationshowfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - Show first and last page buttons in pagination controls

  * `currentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.
When zero, all records will be displayed.

  * `recordsperpage` {`number`} - Number or records per page

  * `resetscrollonpagechange` {`boolean`} - Table will scroll to top left after page change.

  * `select` {`boolean`} - Selection

  * `selectalllabel` {`string`} - Deprecated labels

  * `selectlabel` {`string`} - Label for "select row" checkbox

  * `selected` {`TableData | string[]`} - Can be used to set selected rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to select.

  * `expand` {`boolean`} - When true, will enable row expansion for provided slots.

  * `expandtriggerlabel` {`string`} - Label for "expand row" button. the label is hidden and only used for screen readers.

  * `expandopened` {`TableData`} - Can be used to set expanded rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to expand.

  * `expandpadding` {`RowExpansionPadding`} - Padding for expanded rows. `default` will apply the default padding, `none` will remove padding.

  * `caption` {`string`} - The caption to describe the table can be passed as a property or as a slot.

  * `footer` {`boolean`} - Show a table footer

  * `headerstickyviewport` {`boolean`} - Makes the sticky header attach to the viewport instead of an inner scroll container.

  * `footersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the footer row "sticky"

  * `selectsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row selector column "sticky"

  * `expandsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row expansion column "sticky"

  * `loading` {`boolean | undefined`} - Puts the table into a loading state. This state also prevents the user from performing any interactions with the table

  * `loadinglabel` {`string | undefined`} - Label for the loading indicator when table is in loading state

  * `hidecolumns` {`string[]`} - Column Ids to hide.

  * `stateslotheight` {`string`} - Height of state slot in the table body

  * `customstyles` {`string`} - Custom styles for the table

You can disable them by setting the "disableplaceholderfooters" attribute to true.

Should return true or false.

  * `sortchange` - Dispatched when sorting direction on a column changes with a column id and direction as detail

  * `selectchange` - Dispatched when a row is selected/deselected with the current selection state as detail.

  * `expandchange` - Dispatched when a row is expanded/collapsed with the current expansion state as detail.

  * `hidecolumnschange` - Dispatched when a column is hid/shown with the current hidecolumns state as detail.

  * `rowclick` - Dispatched when a row is clicked.

  * `rowmouseenter` - Dispatched when the cursor enters a row.

  * `rowmouseleave` - Dispatched when the cursor leaves a row.

  * `rowselected` - Dispatched when a row is selected

  * `rowdeselected` - Dispatched when a row is deselected

  * `rowexpanded` - Dispatched when a row is expanded

  * `rowcollapsed` - Dispatched when a row is collapsed

  * `columnresizestart` - Dispatched when user starts resizing a column.
detail: { column: string; size: number }

  * `columnresizing` - Dispatched continuously while a column is being resized.
detail: { column: string; size: number }

  * `columnresizeend` - Dispatched when user ends resizing a column.
detail: { column: string; size: number }

  * `getPageCount` - Returns the pagination page count

  * `resetRowSelection` - Resets the row selection to empty state

  * `resetSorting` - Resets sorting to empty state

  * `getSelectedRowDataKeys` - Returns the selected row datakeys

  * `getColumnHeader` - Returns header object for a given columnId.

  * `getColumnData` - Returns data for a column

  * `getVisibleRows` - Returns visible rows, depending if pagination is enabled or not.

  * `langObserver` {`MutationObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `handleDocumentLangChange` - Request update when document lang changes

attributes.description:
`expandtriggerlabel` {`string`} - Label for "expand row" button. the label is hidden and only used for screen readers.

Property: expandtriggerlabel

Default: Expand row

attributes.name: expandopened
attributes.description:
`expandopened` {`TableData`} - Can be used to set expanded rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to expand.

Property: expandopened

Default:

attributes.name: expandpadding
attributes.description:
`expandpadding` {`RowExpansionPadding`} - Padding for expanded rows. `default` will apply the default padding, `none` will remove padding.

Property: expandpadding

Default: default

attributes.name: caption
attributes.description:
`caption` {`string`} - The caption to describe the table can be passed as a property or as a slot.

Property: caption

Default:

attributes.name: footer
attributes.description:
`footer` {`boolean`} - Show a table footer

Property: footer

Default: false

attributes.name: headersticky
attributes.description:
`headersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the header row "sticky"

Property: headersticky

Default: false

attributes.name: headerstickyviewport
attributes.description:
`headerstickyviewport` {`boolean`} - Makes the sticky header attach to the viewport instead of an inner scroll container.
When enabled the table wrapper stops using the `mds-table--scrollable` modifier so the page controls vertical scrolling.

Property: headerstickyviewport

Default: false

attributes.name: headerstickyoffsetselector
attributes.description:
`headerstickyoffsetselector` {`string`} - CSS selector for one or more elements whose combined offset should be respected by the sticky header.
You can pass a selector list (e.g. ".toolbar, #app-header").
The component observes the matched elements for size changes and keeps the table header positioned underneath them.

Property: headerstickyoffsetselector

Default:

attributes.description:
`footersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the footer row "sticky"

Property: footersticky

Default: false

attributes.description:

Property: selectsticky

Default: false

attributes.name: expandsticky
attributes.description:

Property: expandsticky

Default: false

attributes.description:
`hidecolumns` {`string[]`} - Column Ids to hide.

Property: hidecolumns

Default:

attributes.name: stateslotheight
attributes.description:
`stateslotheight` {`string`} - Height of state slot in the table body

Property: stateslotheight

Default: auto

attributes.name: customstyles
attributes.description:
`customstyles` {`string`} - Custom styles for the table

Property: customstyles

Default:

attributes.name: disableroundedcorners
attributes.description:
`disableroundedcorners` {`boolean`} - Disables rounded corners

Property: disableroundedcorners

Default: false

attributes.name: disableplaceholderfooters
attributes.description:
`disableplaceholderfooters` {`boolean`} - By default table will render "placeholder" footers for the select and expand columns.
You can disable them by setting the "disableplaceholderfooters" attribute to true.

Property: disableplaceholderfooters

Default: false

attributes.name: selectrowdisabled
attributes.description:
`selectrowdisabled` - A function to determine if the row selection checkbox should be disabled.
Should return true or false.

Property: selectrowdisabled

Default: undefined

tags.name: mc-table
tags.description:
Purpose: A table to display data in rows and columns with advanced features like sorting, pagination, row selection, and expandable rows.
Category: Data Display, Layout
When to use: When you need to present tabular data with advanced interaction capabilities.
When not to use: For simple lists or when a grid layout is more appropriate.
Works with components: mc-avatar, mc-badge, mc-button, mc-checkbox, mc-icon, mc-input, mc-multi-select, mc-pagination, mc-popover, mc-select, mc-tooltip
Alternative component names: Data Grid, Data Table, Tabular View

Events:

  * `sortchange` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when sorting direction on a column changes with a column id and direction as detail.

  * `selectchange` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is selected/deselected with the current selection state as detail.

  * `expandchange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when a row is expanded/collapsed with the current expansion state as detail.

  * `hidecolumnschange` {`"CustomEvent<string " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when a column is hid/shown with the current hidecolumns state as detail.

  * `paginationpagechange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when the page changes (only when pagination is enabled) with the visible rows as detail.

  * `paginationpagesizechange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when the page size changes.

  * `rowclick` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is clicked.

  * `rowmouseenter` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when the cursor enters a row.

  * `rowmouseleave` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when the cursor leaves a row.

  * `rowselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is selected.

  * `rowdeselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is deselected.

  * `rowexpanded` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is expanded.

  * `rowcollapsed` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is collapsed.

  * `columnresizestart` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when user starts resizing a column.

  * `columnresizing` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched continuously (animation frame throttled) while a column is being resized.

  * `columnresizeend` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when user ends resizing a column.

  * `pagechange` {`CustomEvent<Record<string, string | number | boolean | object>[]>`} - 

Slots:

  * ``{rowId}_{columnId}`` {} - When you want to pass other mc-components or your own custom components to the table cell. The first parameter in the slot name {rowId} is a row id(the component will automatically try to access an \`id\` attribute, you can change that by passing the \`datakey\` attribute), the second {columnId} is the id of the column where custom component should be rendered, i.e. 10_status, 11_status, 12_status, etc

  * ``caption`` {} - Caption to describe the table.

  * ``{columnId}_header`` {} - Header slot for a column with {columnId} as an \`id\` property.

  * ``{columnId}_footer`` {} - Footer slot for a column with {columnId} as an \`id\` property.

  * ``{rowId}_expanded`` {} - Slot for an expandable row content.

  * ``state`` {} - Slot in the table body. Can be used to display state of the table, i.e. loading, empty, error, etc.

Attributes:

  * `datakey` {`string`} - 

  * `zebrastripes` {`boolean`} - 

  * `truncate` {`boolean`} - 

  * `outerborderstyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `verticallinestyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `horizontallinestyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `disablerowhighlightonhover` {`boolean`} - 

  * `verticalalign` {`TableVerticalAlign | undefined`} - 

  * `nowrap` {`boolean`} - 

  * `disableoverflow` {`boolean`} - 

  * `sortdisableoninitialload` {`boolean`} - 

  * `height` {`string`} - 

  * `headerhidden` {`boolean`} - 

  * `labels` {`Partial<IMcTableLabels> | undefined`} - Labels object for customizing component text.
If not provided, labels are automatically loaded based on document's lang attribute.

  * `data` {`TableData`} - An array of objects containing the data to be rendered by the table.

  * `spans` {`SpanConfig[]`} - Configuration for cells that should span multiple rows and/or columns.
Each object in the array specifies which cell should span and how many rows/columns.

  * `columnresize` {`boolean`} - Enable column resizing functionality

  * `columns` {`TableColumn[]`} - An array of `TableColumn` configurations which informs the table how to render a column and it's data cells.
If no columns are passed, the table will infer columns from the first row of data

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the table

  * `sortdefaultcolumnid` {`string`} - Default sort column.
Will default to the first column.

  * `sortdefaultdirection` {`TableSortDirection`} - Default sort direction

  * `sortdisabled` {`boolean`} - Sorting disabled

  * `sortmanual` {`boolean`} - Enables manual sorting for the table. If this is true, you will be expected to sort your data before it is passed to the table. This is useful if you are doing server-side sorting.

  * `pagination` {`TablePagination`} - Pagination

  * `paginationmode` {`TablePaginationMode`} - Pagination mode (static or dynamic), to be used when pagination is enabled
Use 'static' when you pass the whole dataset to the table, and want to use client-side pagination built into the component
Use 'dynamic' when you want to get data set from the server, and call your rest API on each 'pagechange' event.

  * `paginationscrolltotop` {`boolean`} - Scrolls the table to top left after page change.

  * `paginationloading` {`boolean`} - Loading for pagination state (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationalignment` {`TablePaginationAlignment`} - The alignment of the pagination controls (start, end, center)

  * `paginationtotalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

  * `paginationarialabel` {`string`} - Aria label for pagination wrapper (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationcurrentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.

  * `paginationhidepagesize` {`boolean`} - Hide page size selector.

  * `paginationpagesize` {`number`} - Number of records per page

  * `paginationpagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Items to show in the page size select dropdown

  * `paginationhidepagestatus` {`boolean`} - Hide pagination status text (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows")

  * `paginationshowfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - Show first and last page buttons in pagination controls

  * `currentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.
When zero, all records will be displayed.

  * `recordsperpage` {`number`} - Number or records per page

  * `resetscrollonpagechange` {`boolean`} - Table will scroll to top left after page change.

  * `select` {`boolean`} - Selection

  * `selectalllabel` {`string`} - Deprecated labels

  * `selectlabel` {`string`} - Label for "select row" checkbox

  * `selected` {`TableData | string[]`} - Can be used to set selected rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to select.

  * `expand` {`boolean`} - When true, will enable row expansion for provided slots.

  * `expandtriggerlabel` {`string`} - Label for "expand row" button. the label is hidden and only used for screen readers.

  * `expandopened` {`TableData`} - Can be used to set expanded rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to expand.

  * `expandpadding` {`RowExpansionPadding`} - Padding for expanded rows. `default` will apply the default padding, `none` will remove padding.

  * `caption` {`string`} - The caption to describe the table can be passed as a property or as a slot.

  * `footer` {`boolean`} - Show a table footer

  * `headersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the header row "sticky"

  * `headerstickyviewport` {`boolean`} - Makes the sticky header attach to the viewport instead of an inner scroll container.
When enabled the table wrapper stops using the `mds-table--scrollable` modifier so the page controls vertical scrolling.

  * `headerstickyoffsetselector` {`string`} - CSS selector for one or more elements whose combined offset should be respected by the sticky header.
You can pass a selector list (e.g. ".toolbar, #app-header").
The component observes the matched elements for size changes and keeps the table header positioned underneath them.

  * `footersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the footer row "sticky"

  * `selectsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row selector column "sticky"

  * `expandsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row expansion column "sticky"

  * `hidecolumns` {`string[]`} - Column Ids to hide.

  * `stateslotheight` {`string`} - Height of state slot in the table body

  * `customstyles` {`string`} - Custom styles for the table

  * `disableroundedcorners` {`boolean`} - Disables rounded corners

  * `disableplaceholderfooters` {`boolean`} - By default table will render "placeholder" footers for the select and expand columns.
You can disable them by setting the "disableplaceholderfooters" attribute to true.

  * `selectrowdisabled` - A function to determine if the row selection checkbox should be disabled.
Should return true or false.

Properties:

  * `datakey` {`string`} - 

  * `zebrastripes` {`boolean`} - 

  * `truncate` {`boolean`} - 

  * `outerborderstyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `verticallinestyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `horizontallinestyle` {`TableBorderStyle`} - 

  * `disablerowhighlightonhover` {`boolean`} - 

  * `verticalalign` {`TableVerticalAlign | undefined`} - 

  * `nowrap` {`boolean`} - 

  * `disableoverflow` {`boolean`} - 

  * `sortdisableoninitialload` {`boolean`} - 

  * `height` {`string`} - 

  * `headerhidden` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_resizeDebounceTimer` {`number | undefined`} - 

  * `_isResizing` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_preventSortingTimer` {`number | undefined`} - 

  * `_mcPopoverActive` - 

  * `_cellElementWithActivePopovers` {`HTMLElement | null`} - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetObserver` {`ResizeObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetIntersectionObserver` {`IntersectionObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetTargets` {`Element[]`} - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetWindowResizeHandler` - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetScrollHandler` - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetRaf` {`number | undefined`} - 

  * `headerStickyOffsetRetryHandle` {`number | undefined`} - 

  * `headerStickingObserver` {`IntersectionObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `computedColumns` {`ColumnDef<unknown, unknown>[]`} - 

  * `hasCaptionSlot` {`boolean`} - 

  * `hasStateSlot` {`boolean`} - 

  * `headerStickyTop` {`string`} - 

  * `isHeaderSticking` {`boolean`} - 

  * `tableWrapper` {`HTMLElement`} - 

  * `resizeObserver` {`ResizeObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `hadPreviousData` {`boolean`} - 

  * `shouldDispatchSortEvent` {`boolean`} - 

  * `spanCache` {`Map<string, SpanConfig>`} - 

  * `skipRenderCache` {`Set<string>`} - 

  * `hasShownSpanWarning` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_stickyRecalculationNeeded` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_paginationExplicitlySet` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_afterFirstUdpdate` {`boolean`} - 

  * `_sort` {`SortingState`} - 

  * `_pagination` {`PaginationState`} - 

  * `_rowSelection` - 

  * `_expanded` - 

  * `_pinnedColumns` {`ColumnPinningState`} - 

  * `_expandable` {`Map<string, boolean>`} - 

  * `_captionID` {`string`} - 

  * `computedLabels` {`Required<IMcTableLabels>`} - 

  * `controller` - 

  * `isPagiantionEnabled` {`boolean`} - 

  * `labels` {`Partial<IMcTableLabels> | undefined`} - Labels object for customizing component text.
If not provided, labels are automatically loaded based on document's lang attribute.

  * `data` {`TableData`} - An array of objects containing the data to be rendered by the table.

  * `spans` {`SpanConfig[]`} - Configuration for cells that should span multiple rows and/or columns.
Each object in the array specifies which cell should span and how many rows/columns.

  * `columnresize` {`boolean`} - Enable column resizing functionality

  * `columns` {`TableColumn[]`} - An array of `TableColumn` configurations which informs the table how to render a column and it's data cells.
If no columns are passed, the table will infer columns from the first row of data

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the table

  * `sortdefaultcolumnid` {`string`} - Default sort column.
Will default to the first column.

  * `sortdefaultdirection` {`TableSortDirection`} - Default sort direction

  * `sortdisabled` {`boolean`} - Sorting disabled

  * `sortmanual` {`boolean`} - Enables manual sorting for the table. If this is true, you will be expected to sort your data before it is passed to the table. This is useful if you are doing server-side sorting.

  * `pagination` {`TablePagination`} - Pagination

  * `paginationmode` {`TablePaginationMode`} - Pagination mode (static or dynamic), to be used when pagination is enabled
Use 'static' when you pass the whole dataset to the table, and want to use client-side pagination built into the component
Use 'dynamic' when you want to get data set from the server, and call your rest API on each 'pagechange' event.

  * `paginationscrolltotop` {`boolean`} - Scrolls the table to top left after page change.

  * `paginationloading` {`boolean`} - Loading for pagination state (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationalignment` {`TablePaginationAlignment`} - The alignment of the pagination controls (start, end, center)

  * `paginationtotalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

  * `paginationarialabel` {`string`} - Aria label for pagination wrapper (only used when pagination is enabled)

  * `paginationcurrentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.

  * `paginationhidepagesize` {`boolean`} - Hide page size selector.

  * `paginationpagesize` {`number`} - Number of records per page

  * `paginationpagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Items to show in the page size select dropdown

  * `paginationhidepagestatus` {`boolean`} - Hide pagination status text (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows")

  * `paginationshowfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - Show first and last page buttons in pagination controls

  * `currentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.
When zero, all records will be displayed.

  * `recordsperpage` {`number`} - Number or records per page

  * `resetscrollonpagechange` {`boolean`} - Table will scroll to top left after page change.

  * `select` {`boolean`} - Selection

  * `selectalllabel` {`string`} - Deprecated labels

  * `selectlabel` {`string`} - Label for "select row" checkbox

  * `selected` {`TableData | string[]`} - Can be used to set selected rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to select.

  * `expand` {`boolean`} - When true, will enable row expansion for provided slots.

  * `expandtriggerlabel` {`string`} - Label for "expand row" button. the label is hidden and only used for screen readers.

  * `expandopened` {`TableData`} - Can be used to set expanded rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to expand.

  * `expandpadding` {`RowExpansionPadding`} - Padding for expanded rows. `default` will apply the default padding, `none` will remove padding.

  * `caption` {`string`} - The caption to describe the table can be passed as a property or as a slot.

  * `footer` {`boolean`} - Show a table footer

  * `headersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the header row "sticky"

  * `headerstickyviewport` {`boolean`} - Makes the sticky header attach to the viewport instead of an inner scroll container.
When enabled the table wrapper stops using the `mds-table--scrollable` modifier so the page controls vertical scrolling.

  * `headerstickyoffsetselector` {`string`} - CSS selector for one or more elements whose combined offset should be respected by the sticky header.
You can pass a selector list (e.g. ".toolbar, #app-header").
The component observes the matched elements for size changes and keeps the table header positioned underneath them.

  * `footersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the footer row "sticky"

  * `selectsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row selector column "sticky"

  * `expandsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row expansion column "sticky"

  * `loading` {`boolean | undefined`} - Puts the table into a loading state. This state also prevents the user from performing any interactions with the table

  * `loadinglabel` {`string | undefined`} - Label for the loading indicator when table is in loading state

  * `hidecolumns` {`string[]`} - Column Ids to hide.

  * `stateslotheight` {`string`} - Height of state slot in the table body

  * `customstyles` {`string`} - Custom styles for the table

  * `disableroundedcorners` {`boolean`} - Disables rounded corners

  * `disableplaceholderfooters` {`boolean`} - By default table will render "placeholder" footers for the select and expand columns.
You can disable them by setting the "disableplaceholderfooters" attribute to true.

  * `selectrowdisabled` - A function to determine if the row selection checkbox should be disabled.
Should return true or false.

  * `sortchange` - Dispatched when sorting direction on a column changes with a column id and direction as detail

  * `selectchange` - Dispatched when a row is selected/deselected with the current selection state as detail.

  * `expandchange` - Dispatched when a row is expanded/collapsed with the current expansion state as detail.

  * `hidecolumnschange` - Dispatched when a column is hid/shown with the current hidecolumns state as detail.

  * `rowclick` - Dispatched when a row is clicked.

  * `rowmouseenter` - Dispatched when the cursor enters a row.

  * `rowmouseleave` - Dispatched when the cursor leaves a row.

  * `rowselected` - Dispatched when a row is selected

  * `rowdeselected` - Dispatched when a row is deselected

  * `rowexpanded` - Dispatched when a row is expanded

  * `rowcollapsed` - Dispatched when a row is collapsed

  * `columnresizestart` - Dispatched when user starts resizing a column.
detail: { column: string; size: number }

  * `columnresizing` - Dispatched continuously while a column is being resized.
detail: { column: string; size: number }

  * `columnresizeend` - Dispatched when user ends resizing a column.
detail: { column: string; size: number }

  * `getPageCount` - Returns the pagination page count

  * `resetRowSelection` - Resets the row selection to empty state

  * `resetSorting` - Resets sorting to empty state

  * `getSelectedRowDataKeys` - Returns the selected row datakeys

  * `getColumnHeader` - Returns header object for a given columnId.

  * `getColumnData` - Returns data for a column

  * `getVisibleRows` - Returns visible rows, depending if pagination is enabled or not.

  * `langObserver` {`MutationObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `handleDocumentLangChange` - Request update when document lang changes

attributes.name: data
attributes.description:
`data` {`TableData`} - An array of objects containing the data to be rendered by the table.

Property: data

Default:

attributes.name: spans
attributes.description:
`spans` {`SpanConfig[]`} - Configuration for cells that should span multiple rows and/or columns.
Each object in the array specifies which cell should span and how many rows/columns.

Property: spans

Default:

attributes.name: columnresize
attributes.description:
`columnresize` {`boolean`} - Enable column resizing functionality

Property: columnresize

Default: false

attributes.name: columns
attributes.description:
`columns` {`TableColumn[]`} - An array of `TableColumn` configurations which informs the table how to render a column and it's data cells.
If no columns are passed, the table will infer columns from the first row of data

Property: columns

Default:

attributes.name: fit
attributes.description:
`fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the table

Property: fit

Default: medium

attributes.name: sortdefaultcolumnid
attributes.description:
`sortdefaultcolumnid` {`string`} - Default sort column.
Will default to the first column.

Property: sortdefaultcolumnid

Default:

attributes.name: sortdefaultdirection
attributes.description:
`sortdefaultdirection` {`TableSortDirection`} - Default sort direction

Property: sortdefaultdirection

Default: ascending

attributes.name: sortdisabled
attributes.description:
`sortdisabled` {`boolean`} - Sorting disabled

Property: sortdisabled

Default: false

attributes.name: sortmanual
attributes.description:
`sortmanual` {`boolean`} - Enables manual sorting for the table. If this is true, you will be expected to sort your data before it is passed to the table. This is useful if you are doing server-side sorting.

Property: sortmanual

Default: false

attributes.name: pagination
attributes.description:
`pagination` {`TablePagination`} - Pagination

Property: pagination

Default: none

attributes.name: paginationmode
attributes.description:
`paginationmode` {`TablePaginationMode`} - Pagination mode (static or dynamic), to be used when pagination is enabled
Use 'static' when you pass the whole dataset to the table, and want to use client-side pagination built into the component
Use 'dynamic' when you want to get data set from the server, and call your rest API on each 'pagechange' event.

Property: paginationmode

Default: static

attributes.name: paginationscrolltotop
attributes.description:
`paginationscrolltotop` {`boolean`} - Scrolls the table to top left after page change.

Property: paginationscrolltotop

Default: false

attributes.name: paginationloading
attributes.description:
`paginationloading` {`boolean`} - Loading for pagination state (only used when pagination is enabled)

Property: paginationloading

Default: false

attributes.name: paginationalignment
attributes.description:
`paginationalignment` {`TablePaginationAlignment`} - The alignment of the pagination controls (start, end, center)

Property: paginationalignment

Default: center

attributes.name: paginationtotalrecords
attributes.description:
`paginationtotalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

Property: paginationtotalrecords

Default: 0

attributes.name: paginationarialabel
attributes.description:
`paginationarialabel` {`string`} - Aria label for pagination wrapper (only used when pagination is enabled)

Property: paginationarialabel

Default: Navigate Table Rows

attributes.name: paginationcurrentpage
attributes.description:
`paginationcurrentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.

Property: paginationcurrentpage

Default: 1

attributes.name: paginationhidepagesize
attributes.description:
`paginationhidepagesize` {`boolean`} - Hide page size selector.

Property: paginationhidepagesize

Default: false

attributes.name: paginationpagesize
attributes.description:
`paginationpagesize` {`number`} - Number of records per page

Property: paginationpagesize

Default: 10

attributes.name: paginationpagesizeoptions
attributes.description:
`paginationpagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Items to show in the page size select dropdown

Property: paginationpagesizeoptions

Default: 10,20,50,100

attributes.name: paginationhidepagestatus
attributes.description:
`paginationhidepagestatus` {`boolean`} - Hide pagination status text (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows")

Property: paginationhidepagestatus

Default: false

attributes.name: paginationshowfirstandlastpage
attributes.description:
`paginationshowfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - Show first and last page buttons in pagination controls

Property: paginationshowfirstandlastpage

Default: false

attributes.name: currentpage
attributes.description:
`currentpage` {`number`} - Current page to show from the data past to the table.
When zero, all records will be displayed.

Property: currentpage

Default: 0

attributes.name: recordsperpage
attributes.description:
`recordsperpage` {`number`} - Number or records per page

Property: recordsperpage

Default: 20

attributes.name: resetscrollonpagechange
attributes.description:
`resetscrollonpagechange` {`boolean`} - Table will scroll to top left after page change.

Property: resetscrollonpagechange

Default: false

attributes.name: select
attributes.description:
`select` {`boolean`} - Selection

Property: select

Default: false

attributes.name: selectalllabel
attributes.description:
`selectalllabel` {`string`} - Deprecated labels

Property: selectalllabel

Default: Select all rows

attributes.name: selectlabel
attributes.description:
`selectlabel` {`string`} - Label for "select row" checkbox

Property: selectlabel

Default: Select row

attributes.name: selected
attributes.description:
`selected` {`TableData | string[]`} - Can be used to set selected rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to select.

Property: selected

Default:

attributes.name: expand
attributes.description:
`expand` {`boolean`} - When true, will enable row expansion for provided slots.

Property: expand

Default: false

attributes.name: expandtriggerlabel
attributes.description:
`expandtriggerlabel` {`string`} - Label for "expand row" button. the label is hidden and only used for screen readers.

Property: expandtriggerlabel

Default: Expand row

attributes.name: expandopened
attributes.description:
`expandopened` {`TableData`} - Can be used to set expanded rows programatically. Pass data objects that you want to expand.

Property: expandopened

Default:

attributes.name: expandpadding
attributes.description:
`expandpadding` {`RowExpansionPadding`} - Padding for expanded rows. `default` will apply the default padding, `none` will remove padding.

Property: expandpadding

Default: default

attributes.name: caption
attributes.description:
`caption` {`string`} - The caption to describe the table can be passed as a property or as a slot.

Property: caption

Default:

attributes.name: footer
attributes.description:
`footer` {`boolean`} - Show a table footer

Property: footer

Default: false

attributes.name: headersticky
attributes.description:
`headersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the header row "sticky"

Property: headersticky

Default: false

attributes.name: headerstickyviewport
attributes.description:
`headerstickyviewport` {`boolean`} - Makes the sticky header attach to the viewport instead of an inner scroll container.
When enabled the table wrapper stops using the `mds-table--scrollable` modifier so the page controls vertical scrolling.

Property: headerstickyviewport

Default: false

attributes.name: headerstickyoffsetselector
attributes.description:
`headerstickyoffsetselector` {`string`} - CSS selector for one or more elements whose combined offset should be respected by the sticky header.
You can pass a selector list (e.g. ".toolbar, #app-header").
The component observes the matched elements for size changes and keeps the table header positioned underneath them.

Property: headerstickyoffsetselector

Default:

attributes.name: footersticky
attributes.description:
`footersticky` {`boolean`} - Make the footer row "sticky"

Property: footersticky

Default: false

attributes.name: selectsticky
attributes.description:
`selectsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row selector column "sticky"

Property: selectsticky

Default: false

attributes.name: expandsticky
attributes.description:
`expandsticky` {`boolean`} - Make the row expansion column "sticky"

Property: expandsticky

Default: false

attributes.name: hidecolumns
attributes.description:
`hidecolumns` {`string[]`} - Column Ids to hide.

Property: hidecolumns

Default:

attributes.name: stateslotheight
attributes.description:
`stateslotheight` {`string`} - Height of state slot in the table body

Property: stateslotheight

Default: auto

attributes.name: customstyles
attributes.description:
`customstyles` {`string`} - Custom styles for the table

Property: customstyles

Default:

attributes.name: disableroundedcorners
attributes.description:
`disableroundedcorners` {`boolean`} - Disables rounded corners

Property: disableroundedcorners

Default: false

attributes.name: disableplaceholderfooters
attributes.description:
`disableplaceholderfooters` {`boolean`} - By default table will render "placeholder" footers for the select and expand columns.
You can disable them by setting the "disableplaceholderfooters" attribute to true.

Property: disableplaceholderfooters

Default: false

attributes.name: selectrowdisabled
attributes.description:
`selectrowdisabled` - A function to determine if the row selection checkbox should be disabled.
Should return true or false.

Property: selectrowdisabled

Default: undefined

attributes.name: onsortchange
attributes.description: `sortchange` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when sorting direction on a column changes with a column id and direction as detail.

attributes.name: onselectchange
attributes.description: `selectchange` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is selected/deselected with the current selection state as detail.

attributes.name: onexpandchange
attributes.description: `expandchange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when a row is expanded/collapsed with the current expansion state as detail.

attributes.name: onhidecolumnschange
attributes.description: `hidecolumnschange` {`"CustomEvent<string " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when a column is hid/shown with the current hidecolumns state as detail.

attributes.name: onpaginationpagechange
attributes.description: `paginationpagechange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when the page changes (only when pagination is enabled) with the visible rows as detail.

attributes.name: onpaginationpagesizechange
attributes.description: `paginationpagesizechange` {`"CustomEvent<unknown " | " undefined>"`} - Dispatched when the page size changes.

attributes.name: onrowclick
attributes.description: `rowclick` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is clicked.

attributes.name: onrowmouseenter
attributes.description: `rowmouseenter` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when the cursor enters a row.

attributes.name: onrowmouseleave
attributes.description: `rowmouseleave` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when the cursor leaves a row.

attributes.name: onrowselected
attributes.description: `rowselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is selected.

attributes.name: onrowdeselected
attributes.description: `rowdeselected` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is deselected.

attributes.name: onrowexpanded
attributes.description: `rowexpanded` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is expanded.

attributes.name: onrowcollapsed
attributes.description: `rowcollapsed` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when a row is collapsed.

attributes.name: oncolumnresizestart
attributes.description: `columnresizestart` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when user starts resizing a column.

attributes.name: oncolumnresizing
attributes.description: `columnresizing` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched continuously (animation frame throttled) while a column is being resized.

attributes.name: oncolumnresizeend
attributes.description: `columnresizeend` {`CustomEvent<any>`} - Dispatched when user ends resizing a column.

attributes.name: onpagechange
attributes.description: `pagechange` {`CustomEvent<Record<string, string | number | boolean | object>[]>`} -
